title: DB migration + Task/TaskDecomposition models
context: Task 1 of 3 for goal-to-plan decomposition. See full design doc: docs/goal-to-plan-decomposition-design.md, Section 3 (Data Model) and Section 7 (Task 1). Add parent_task_id + subtask_order columns to tasks table. Create task_decompositions table. Update SQLAlchemy models and Pydantic schemas.
acceptance: Migration runs clean (python -m pytest -v passes, no table errors). Task model has parent_task_id (nullable FK to tasks.id) and subtask_order (nullable int). TaskDecomposition model has all fields from design doc section 3.2. Pydantic schemas updated: TaskBase + Task include parent_task_id and subtask_order; new TaskDecompositionCreate, TaskDecompositionUpdate, TaskDecomposition schemas. Existing task tests unchanged.

title: DecompositionService — LLM call + commit logic
context: Task 2 of 3 for goal-to-plan decomposition. Depends on Task 1 (migration + models). See design doc section 5 (DecompositionService) and section 7 (Task 2). Implement app/services/decomposition.py. Uses same Gateway sessions_spawn pattern as app/orchestrator/auto_assigner.py — study that file for the LLM call pattern.
acceptance: generate() calls LLM via Gateway, saves TaskDecomposition(status=pending), returns DecompositionPreview with parent_task and subtasks list. commit() creates parent Task + subtask Tasks with parent_task_id set and subtask_order set; converts depends_on indices to blocked_by task IDs; updates decomposition record to committed. discard() sets status=discarded. _parse_llm_response() handles clean JSON, ```json-wrapped JSON, and raises on unparseable. Unit tests for _parse_llm_response() and depends_on→blocked_by conversion. Integration test for generate() with mocked Gateway.

spec.llm_pattern: Follow app/orchestrator/auto_assigner.py _choose_agent_llm() pattern: sessions_spawn then poll sessions_history up to 45s (9 polls x 5s)
prompt_requirements: Schema: { parent_task: {title, notes, agent, shape}, subtasks: [{title, notes, agent, order, depends_on}], rationale: string }
agent must be one of: programmer, writer, reviewer, researcher, architect
depends_on is list of order integers (1-based) for blocking subtasks

commit_logic.blocked_by_conversion: For each subtask, depends_on=[1,3] means it's blocked by the tasks created for order=1 and order=3. Map order index to generated task UUID.
commit_logic.parent_task_fields: status defaults to inbox, owner defaults to lobs, project_id from decomposition record
commit_logic.subtask_fields: Same as parent but with parent_task_id=parent.id and subtask_order=order value from plan

error_handling.invalid_json: raise HTTPException 503 with detail explaining parse failure

title: API router — decompose endpoints
context: Task 3 of 3 for goal-to-plan decomposition. Depends on Tasks 1 and 2. See design doc section 4 (API Specification) and section 7 (Task 3). Add decompose endpoints to tasks router or create a new app/routers/decompose.py. Wire into app/main.py.
acceptance: POST /api/tasks/decompose returns 200 with parent_task and subtasks preview. POST /api/tasks/decompose/{id}/commit returns 201 with parent_task_id and subtask_ids. DELETE /api/tasks/decompose/{id} returns 200. GET /api/tasks/decompose/{id} returns decomposition record. Round-trip integration test: generate → commit → tasks exist in DB with correct parent_task_id and blocked_by. Double-commit returns 409. Bad project_id on generate returns 404.

endpoints.request_body: DecomposeRequest { goal_text: str, project_id: str, context: str | None }
endpoints.response: DecompositionPreview { decomposition_id: str, parent_task: {...}, subtasks: [...], rationale: str }
endpoints.notes: Validate project_id exists. goal_text must be non-empty.
Validate decomposition exists and is pending. Validate subtask depends_on indices are valid. Check for dependency cycles.

spec.main_py_addition: from app.routers import decompose; app.include_router(decompose.router, prefix='/api', dependencies=[Depends(require_auth)])
spec.cycle_detection: Simple DFS on depends_on graph. If cycle detected, return 422 with which indices form the cycle.
